evals.prompt: Is demand for my keywords growing or shrinking year over year? Tell me where to invest and where to exit.
evals.expected_output: Should compute, per keyword, YoY growth (last 12 months vs prior 12 when 24 months exist) and a normalized trend slope (% per month), classify each as Rising/Declining/Stable/Emerging/Fading, roll up a portfolio rising-vs-declining volume share, and produce invest (double down) and exit (phase out) recommendations. Saves a dated JSON report and reports 0 NodesHub tokens.
assertions: Triggers on demand-trend / is-this-keyword-growing / invest-or-exit / yoy language
Computes YoY growth (last 12 months vs prior 12) when 24 months are available
Computes a least-squares trend slope normalized as percent per month
Classifies each keyword as Rising, Declining, Stable, Emerging, or Fading
Produces a portfolio summary with rising-vs-declining volume share
Produces invest (double down) and exit (phase out) recommendations

evals.prompt: Run the demand trajectory analyzer on the demo data and show me the rising and declining topics.
evals.expected_output: Should run analyze.py --demo against the built-in fixture (a rising topic, a declining topic, a stable one, and a spiky-but-flat one), print the per-keyword table with YoY and slope, label the rising and declining topics correctly, and crucially classify the spiky-but-flat keyword as Stable rather than mistaking its seasonal swing for a trend.
assertions: Runs with the --demo flag and needs no API key or data file
Prints a per-keyword table with latest 12m volume, YoY, slope, and classification
Labels the rising topic Rising and the declining topic Declining
Classifies the spiky-but-flat keyword as Stable (deseasonalization works)
Does not call the NodesHub API

evals.prompt: I have a CSV with 18 months of monthly search volumes per keyword. Classify each topic's trajectory even though I do not have a full 24 months.
evals.expected_output: Should ingest the CSV via --series (long format keyword,date,volume), and because YoY needs 24 months, fall back to the trend slope to classify short-history keywords as Emerging (steep positive) or Fading (steep negative) or Stable. Falls back to a clear DataForSEO setup message if --source dfs is requested without credentials.
assertions: Reads per-keyword dated monthly volumes from the CSV via --series
Handles a short (~18 month) history where YoY is unavailable
Uses the trend slope to classify short-history keywords (Emerging / Fading / Stable)
If DataForSEO is requested without DATAFORSEO_LOGIN/PASSWORD, prints a clear setup message and exits gracefully
